articles.headline: TO THE PUBLIC / To our Readers
articles.author: Committee of Publication (James Wallace, Secretary)
articles.summary: Announcement that the Colored American has passed from its original proprietor into the hands of a large Committee of Publication of colored citizens of New York, who now select the editor, agents and printers. Declares the paper 'the organ of the colored people of the United States' and appeals for support: 'If OUR PRESS live not, and flourish not among us, and BY US, we cannot live in America, neither morally, intellectually, nor civilly.' Notes the editor received only $10 for a year's service. Signed by about thirty men including Thomas L. Jinnings, Samuel Cornish, Henry Sipkins, Theodore S. Wright, Thomas Jackson, Jacob Hendricks, James Wallace (secretary), Joseph Harris, Wm. P. Johnson, William Wilkins and others. Dated New-York, Jan. 8, 1838.

articles.headline: LECTURES OF THE PHILOMATHEAN SOCIETY
articles.summary: The Philomathean Society's lecture season opens Tuesday evening the 26th at Philomathean Hall, 161 Duane Street, New York. Course: Chemistry by Dr. John Brown (3 lectures); Organs of Sense by James McCune Smith, M.D. (4); Oratorical Delivery by R. F. Wake (4); Evidences of Christianity by I. G. DeGrasse, A.B. (3); History by P. A. Bell (4); Phrenology by J. Peterson (1); [subject] by S. V. Berry (2); Decision of Character by James Field (2). Season tickets $1; single 12.5 cents.

articles.headline: NOTICE — State Temperance Society of Connecticut
articles.author: H. Foster, Pres't; A. G. Beman, Rec. Sec.
articles.summary: The fourth meeting of the Colored Americans' State Temperance Society of Connecticut will be held in Hartford on the 17th of April 1838; friends of total abstinence invited.

articles.headline: Ohio Memorial — Extract No. 5 (Concluded): Trinidad / Brazil
articles.summary: Concluding extract of the Ohio memorial for equal laws: cites free people of color prospering in Trinidad (15,000 free, no paupers) and Brazil (600,000 enfranchised persons among merchants, farmers, doctors, lawyers, officers), plus Gov. Giles of Virginia's 1827 letter admitting low conviction rates among free Black Virginians; closes with a prayer for immediate repeal of all laws that harass, degrade and injure colored citizens.

events.name: Colored American transferred to Committee of Publication
events.description: Formation of the proprietor committee of the Colored American newspaper.

events.name: Philomathean Society lecture course
events.location: Philomathean Hall, 161 Duane St., New York
events.description: Lecture season of the (colored) Philomathean Society.

events.name: State Temperance Society of Connecticut, 4th meeting
events.description: Fourth meeting of the Colored Americans' State Temperance Society of Connecticut.

agents.note: Stephen H. Gloucester — Agent of the Colored American for Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland and West Jersey; office on Lombard Street, Philadelphia.
